Use: When an Acknowledgement is used as a stand-alone product, it is simply an add-on to a notary's normal process; the IDN will not relate to any other Registrations; there will not be any image scanned and uploaded
Related Data:
Full IDN Data: title, pages, signer(s), date, Illuminotary, address, location, location detail, and others present
** n.b. Full IDN Data to be collected for all Documents
Assembly:
* The customer brings the document for notarizing
* Acknowledgement generated through DARCI
* Acknowledgement becomes the final page of the document and leaves with the customer
Result:
DARCI retains Full IDN Data; public can search Basic IDN Data through DARCI; no related image
** n.b. every Document/IDN to include this functionality

Use: This document is for use when Member wants to register a deed (or other document by which Member obtained ownership) that has already been executed and recorded sometime in the past.
Related Data:
Property Data: Address and Tax ID from local government (in California, this is called "APN")
Data about the previously executed and recorded document: the record date, record number, and vesting information.
Assembly:
* Registration & Notice generated through DARCI
* An acknowledgement page is included as final page of the document
Result: DARCI retains all data and an image of the Registration & Notice

Use: This document is for use when Member is registering a deed (or other document by which Member is obtaining ownership) that has yet to be executed and recorded.
Related Data: Property Data: Address and Tax ID from local government (in California, this is called an "Assesor's Parcel Number" or "APN")
Assembly:
* Member supplies the document
* The acknowledgement page is generated through DARCI
* The acknowledgement page becomes the final page of the document, which Illuminotary keeps, scans, and uploads
Result: DARCI retains all data and an image of the Document

Use: RRR" stands for "Restatement, Republication, and Registration" This is the document by which a Trust is registered on DARCI and, once assembled, contains all the terms of the Trust.
Related Data: Full Trust Data (all fields set forth in the Trust table), Grantor(s), Trustee(s), and Successor Trustee(s), plus date, pages, and title for each prior Trust document
Assembly:
* RRR generated through DARCI
* An acknowledgement page is included as final page of the Document
* Member to bring all prior Trust documents for inclusion in RRR
* Illuminotary to scan and upload all as one Document
Result: DARCI retains all data and an image of the RRR

Use: The Trust Certification contains all of the Trust information that is relevant to third parties doing business with the trustee and must remain current. Therefore, we will generate a new Trust Certification when the Trust is registered and anytime that a new document is registered to the Trust
Related Data: Full Trust Data (all fields set forth in the Trust table), Grantor(s), Trustee(s), and Successor Trustee(s)
Assembly:
* Trust Certification generated through DARCI
* The acknowledgement page is included as final page of the Document, which Illuminotary keeps, scans, and uploads
Result: DARCI retains all data and an image of the Trust Certification

Use: This Document Type is for use when Member is registering any subsequent Trust document, like an amendment or change of trustee
Related Data: Full Trust Data (all fields set forth in the Trust table), Grantor(s), Trustee(s), and Successor Trustee(s)
Assembly:
* Member supplies the document
* The acknowledgement page is generated through DARCI
* The acknowledgement page becomes the final page of the Document, which Illuminotary keeps, scans, and uploads
Result: DARCI retains all data and an image of the Document

Use: The Power of Attorney document is the document by which a principal names an agent who is authorized to act on the principal's behalf
Related Data: Full POA Data (all fields set forth in the POA table), Principal, Agent(s), and Successor Agent(s)
Assembly:
* POA generated through DARCI
* The acknowledgement page is included as final page of the Document, which Illuminotary keeps, scans, and uploads
Result: DARCI retains all data and an image of the POA

Use: This Document Type is for use when Member is registering any subsequent POA document, such as a resignation or letter of incapacity
Related Data: Full POA Data (all fields set forth in the POA table), Principal, Agent(s), and Successor Agent(s)
Assembly:
* Member supplies the document
* The acknowledgement page is generated through DARCI
* The acknowledgement page becomes the final page of the document, which Illuminotary keeps, scans, and uploads
Result: DARCI retains all data and an image of the Document
